comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: FORCE_CLOSE: close gate(s) bypassed by user approval — no completion/signature attestation was signed. Reason: "REB-659 deleted in Jira (HTTP 204); live reconcile-check now reports 0 unbound Jira issues (677 bindings, 677 in sync, 0 discrepancies). bridge-fsck's OFFLINE prev_snapshot.json is still stale (refreshed by the next scheduled live Reconcile Bridge pass), so its report lags — but the drift is genuinely gone. Latent enabler (empty rebar-id: marker traps a key in unbound limbo) fixed separately in Gerrit change 313 (rite-bile-slate). Closing; the canary will confirm 0 drift on its next live run.". Session: 210396a7f.

compiled_state.description:
## Reproduction Steps

Run `rebar bridge-fsck` — the offline binding-drift arm (epic 3006-e198,
the convergence parity oracle) reports a non-empty `binding_drift` section.

## Expected vs Actual

- **Expected:** `binding_drift` is empty (every bound pair converged).
- **Actual:** unbound_jira=1
- **Detected at:** 2026-07-05T03:15:02Z
- **Canary run:** https://github.com/navapbc/rebar/actions/runs/28727935825

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Each reported drift is triaged (would_terminal → local archive/delete
      to propagate; dangling → confirmed 404 GC; unbound_jira → adopt or
      archive the Jira-native issue; local_gone → investigate).
- [ ] `rebar bridge-fsck` reports an empty `binding_drift` section.

This ticket auto-closes when bridge-fsck next reports zero binding drift.
compiled_state.title: [binding-drift] bridge-fsck found 1 unhealed binding drift(s)
